articles.title: INTERACTION
articles.summary: Understanding your customers isn't just about knowing who they are. It's about understanding what they do. Event data (AKA behavioral data) tells the full story of what your customers want to do with your product, and what they manage to achieve.

articles.title: * Building a strong foundation for user behavior analysis
TRUMP'S TIKTOK PLAN UPENDED BY CHINESE OBJECTIONS OVER TARIFFS
articles.summary: China has withheld approval for a deal that involved the launch of a new US-based version of TikTok owned and operated by a majority of American investors. White House officials had planned for President Trump to sign an executive order approving the proposal, which had the blessings of investors and ByteDance. Trump's recent tariffs have angered China and prompted retaliatory measures against the US. The deadline to reach a deal has been extended by another 75 days.

articles.title: META INTRODUCES LLAMA 4 WITH TWO NEW AI MODELS AVAILABLE NOW, AND TWO MORE ON THE WAY
articles.summary: Meta's new Llama 4 model suite includes four new models, Maverick, Scout, Behemoth, and Reasoning. Both Maverick and Scout are available to download now from the Llama website and Hugging Face and have been added to Meta AI. Behemoth is an upcoming model that Meta claims is among the world's smartest large language models. More information about Llama 4 Reasoning will be released in the next month.

articles.title: THIS BRAIN-COMPUTER INTERFACE IS NOW A TWO-WAY STREET
articles.summary: Scientists at Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research have developed a technology that allows brain-computer interfaces to provide touch feedback to users. The technology was implanted into a patient who was paralyzed from the chest down after a driving accident. It allowed the patient not only to control movements in his hand but also to receive touch feedback and 'feel', using those sensations to guide movements. The patient was able to pick up a cup and drink from it just by thinking about performing the task and pick up an empty eggshell without cracking it.

articles.title: THE FIRST PEOPLE TO FLY IN SPACE OVER THE POLES GOT THIS STUNNING VIEW
articles.summary: Chun Wang, Jannicke Mikkelsen, Rabea Rogge, and Eric Philips were the first people to fly over Earth's poles in space. The private mission launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center on a Falcon 9 rocket on March 31. While these were the first people to make the journey, low-Earth polar orbits are commonly used for weather-tracking and spy satellites. Photos and video from the mission are available in the article.

articles.title: SPARKS
articles.summary: Sparks is a typeface for creating sparklines in text without code. It uses OpenType's contextual alternates feature to perform simple replacement operations on numbers. Sparks works on desktop and on the web without JavaScript (but it requires a browser that can make use of OpenType features in text). It is currently compatible with Microsoft Word 2010 and later, Apple Pages, Adobe Creative Cloud applications, Chrome 33+, Safari 6+, Firefox 4+, and Internet Explorer 10+.

articles.title: THE CURVE IS BENDING
articles.summary: Model output is now actually worth more than the cost of real dev work. It's starting to make sense to throw a lot of money towards inference. This will probably cause some disruption in the junior dev job market - or at least disrupt what it means to be a junior dev. Code-writing agents are predicted to essentially match college graduates in almost all software tasks by the end of 2026.

articles.title: FOUR YEARS OF RUNNING A SAAS IN A COMPETITIVE MARKET
articles.summary: OnlineOrNot was launched in 2021 when there were more than 200 listed alternatives to the tool its creator wanted to create. There are even more competitors today. OnlineOrNot is a self-funded and sustainable business. This post looks at the lessons learned by its creator throughout the lifetime of the business.

articles.title: OPENAI SAYS IT'LL RELEASE O3 AFTER ALL, DELAYS GPT-5
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to release both o3 and o4-mini in a couple of weeks. The company had previously canceled the launch of its o3 reasoning model but reversed course due to its upcoming GPT-5 model. GPT-5 will now roll out later than originally anticipated as the company prepares enough capacity to support what it expects to be unprecedented demand. The model will incorporate voice, Canvas, search, deep research, and more.

articles.summary: AI-driven advertising that generates 3.5X ROI? 1700% valuation increase over three years? No wonder Fortune 500 insiders are rushing to back RAD Intel. The share price changes on 4/10, invest now to lock

articles.title: TYPO MINIMIZING KEYBOARD
articles.summary: This page presents a theoretical keyboard layout that minimizes the number of valid typos.

articles.title: APPLE IPHONE PRICE HIKES ARE NOW LOOKING POSSIBLE IN THE US
articles.summary: Apple's $999 starting price level is a psychological threshold that many consumers probably don't want to cross.

articles.title: OPEN-SOURCE IS JUST THAT
articles.summary: Open source just means that the source is open - it doesn't mean that it's open to contributions, that support is offered, that users are entitled to feature requests or developer time - or anything, and it doesn't mean that it's free.

articles.title: NVIDIA FINALLY ADDS NATIVE PYTHON SUPPORT TO CUDA
articles.summary: Developers can now use Python to directly execute algorithmic-style computing on GPUs.

articles.title: THE AGE OF ABUNDANCE
articles.summary: AI will create a future where we will be able to solve almost all problems, but figuring out how we distribute limited resources is an unsolved question.

articles.title: DOGE REPORTEDLY PLANNING A HACKATHON TO BUILD ‘MEGA API' FOR IRS DATA
articles.summary: Two DOGE staffers, Gavin Kliger and Sam Corcos, plan to launch a hackathon to build an API that would grant cloud providers easy access to (and export of) IRS data including taxpayer names, addresses, social security numbers, tax returns, and employment information.
